original_reasoning: ناموس=law/divine order; νεμεσήσεται=he will be indignant (divine retribution). Strong semantic link
epiboētheō=help/send aid; b3th=send/wake. Strong semantic and phonetic match.
Arabic faz' = 'terror, panic, fleeing for refuge'; Greek phyza = 'panicked rout'. Clear semantic match of panic/terror-induced flight.

الجدي=young goat (also constellation Capricorn). αἰγίς=goat-skin (aegis). Both derive from the goat concept. الجدي IS a young goat, and αἰγίς is literally goat-hide. This is a strong masadiq match in the goat/caprine domain.
Arabic root includes obscene or disordered speech, and Greek means spoken/said; both meet on the core of uttered speech.
Greek 'aim/shoot at' matches the action side that yields the Arabic slain victim/result.
هرقل=Heraclius (Byzantine emperor); Ἡρακλέους=Heracles. Direct Greek name loan via Roman emperor
Arabic warmth/heat can naturally yield softening; Greek 'soften, knead' fits that action-result chain.
